# Ridgeline Collaborative

> Independent policy research for the Pacific Northwest energy transition.

Ridgeline Collaborative is a 501(c)(4) social welfare organization producing rigorous, accessible analysis of the Pacific Northwest's energy transition. We connect technical complexity with public understanding — translating the forces reshaping our electricity system into analysis that policymakers, advocates, and the public can act on.

## Projects

### PNW Energy Reckoning
A comprehensive analysis of three interlocking crises facing the Pacific Northwest electricity system — demand growth, institutional fragmentation, and the clean energy transition. Paper forthcoming 2026.
URL: https://ridgelinecollab.org/reckoning

### Climate & Change Camp 2026
A four-day gathering at River Farm, 80 acres east of Bellingham, WA. 50-80 people working on climate and societal change — futures exercises, Cascadia-specific sessions, nature, and real connection. July 2026.
URL: https://ridgelinecollab.org/camp

## Contact
